channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Justice Department says Biden DOJ weaponized law to go after anti-abortion activists
item.description: The Trump administration has said that enforcement of the FACE Act by the Biden DOJ represents "the prototypical example" of the weaponization of the law against conservatives.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration has said that enforcement of the FACE Act by the Biden DOJ represents "the prototypical example" of the weaponization of the law against conservatives.

(Image credit: Amanda Andrade-Rhoades)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Law enforcement is trying to combat abusive AI. Experts say easier said than done
item.description: An Ohio man was convicted of cybercrimes involving obscene AI-generated images of women and children. But experts warn of the difficulties in going after such cases.
item.content:encoded:
An Ohio man was convicted of cybercrimes involving obscene AI-generated images of women and children. But experts warn of the difficulties in going after such cases.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to start peace negotiations after rare talks in D.C.
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the talks were meant to lay out a framework for lasting peace and to bring an end to Hezbollah's influence in the region.
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the talks were meant to lay out a framework for lasting peace and to bring an end to Hezbollah's influence in the region.

(Image credit: Kevin Lamarque)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Months after removal, Trump administration agrees to let Pride flag fly at Stonewall
item.description: Months after removing the Pride flag from the birthplace of the modern LGBTQ+ rights movement, the Trump administration is allowing the flag to fly once again at Stonewall National Monument.
item.content:encoded: Months after removing the Pride flag from the birthplace of the modern LGBTQ+ rights movement, the Trump administration is allowing the flag to fly once again at Stonewall National Monument.
item.dc:creator: Steve Kastenbaum

item.title: After backlash, Trump deletes post depicting him as Jesus-like figure
item.description: President Trump has now deleted a controversial image that he claims wasn't a depiction of him as Jesus, but rather a doctor.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has now deleted a controversial image that he claims wasn't a depiction of him as Jesus, but rather a doctor.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Man accused in Molotov cocktail attack of OpenAI CEO's home charged with attempted murder
item.description: No one was injured at Altman's home or the company offices, authorities said.
item.content:encoded:
No one was injured at Altman's home or the company offices, authorities said.

(Image credit: Jeff Chiu)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Sexual misconduct on Capitol Hill: The problem that won't go away
item.description: NPR'S Mary Louise speaks with PBS NewsHour correspondent Lisa Desjardins about sexual misconduct allegations against Rep. Eric Swalwell and the pattern of such allegations against members of Congress.
item.content:encoded: NPR'S Mary Louise speaks with PBS NewsHour correspondent Lisa Desjardins about sexual misconduct allegations against Rep. Eric Swalwell and the pattern of such allegations against members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Dorning

item.title: Judge dismisses Trump's $10B lawsuit over the Wall Street Journal's Epstein reporting
item.description: In the order issued Monday, the judge wrote that President Trump had failed to make the argument that the article, which described a letter to Epstein that the newspaper said bore Trump's signature, was published with the intent to be malicious.
item.content:encoded:
In the order issued Monday, the judge wrote that President Trump had failed to make the argument that the article, which described a letter to Epstein that the newspaper said bore Trump's signature, was published with the intent to be malicious.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump vows to sink Iranian ships approaching a U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump said the U.S. would interdict vessels that had paid what he called an "illegal toll" to Iran to cross the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said the U.S. would interdict vessels that had paid what he called an "illegal toll" to Iran to cross the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: More than a quarter of private colleges are at risk of closing, a new projection shows
item.description: As one Vermont college finishes its last semester, an estimated 442 others may be in trouble.
item.content:encoded:
As one Vermont college finishes its last semester, an estimated 442 others may be in trouble.

(Image credit: Oliver Parini for The Hechinger Report)
item.dc:creator: Jon Marcus
